title: Repository-wide continuity validation enforces every relation guarantee
summary: This record materializes the natural-language claim previously stored directly inside CM-1000.contradicts: that one repository-wide continuity validator demonstrably enforces every relation guarantee implemented by both validator modules. It is retained as historical project memory rather than deleted. CM-1000 now contradicts and supersedes this overbroad claim, while CM-1001 supersedes the earlier validator-fragmentation state. Runtime CI verification remains separate and unresolved.

status_relations.observed: CM-1000 stored this claim as prose in a machine-relation array instead of as a linked continuity record.
status_relations.inferred: Leaving prose in relation arrays prevents stable graph traversal and causes strict target-existence validation to fail.
status_relations.proposed: Represent machine relations only as CM record IDs and preserve explanatory wording in summaries or narrative relation notes.
status_relations.superseded: The unqualified claim is retained here as historical content and is superseded by CM-1000's current linked relation and CM-1001's composed-validator evidence.
status_relations.unresolved: A completed CI run at the migration commit has not yet been observed.

falsification_or_review_route: Fetch CM-1000 at blob 131fa02930e7fd2314aaea6f1e649e1f7022af0a and verify the original prose value. Falsify this migration record by showing that the quoted claim was not present, that CM-1000 did not use it as a contradiction target, or that the current linked record does not preserve the same claim. This record preserves claim history; it does not prove CI execution.
